_comment: Tunables for the review scoring system. Read by scrapers/score_ledger.py. Safe to commit (no secrets). See CLAUDE_content_standards_section.md for the rubric these settings govern.
_critic_weight_help: How much scraped/external critic scores (CoffeeReview etc.) may influence OUR score. 'advisory' (default) = flag divergences, change NOTHING automatically. 'low' = also record a bounded suggested nudge for manual review, but still never overwrites our score. The external number is evidence, not a verdict.
_agreement_threshold_help: If |our_score - normalized_critic_score| <= this, we agree. Keep ours, no flag.
_divergence_threshold_help: If |our_score - normalized_critic_score| > this, flag 'score_divergence' in the ledger for manual review. Never auto-adjust.
_max_nudge_help: Hard cap on how far the external score may ever move ours, and only when critic_weight='low'. Recorded as a suggestion; the human applies it.
_comparable_k_help: How many closest prior beans (same roast / sensory / price tier) to inject into the scoring prompt as comparative anchors.
_critic_scale_help: Heuristic map from a 100-pt critic score to our 1-10 scale, FOR DIVERGENCE COMPARISON ONLY (never stored as our score): normalized = clamp((raw - offset) / divisor, min, max). e.g. 90->8.0, 95->9.0, 85->7.0. CoffeeReview only reviews specialty coffee and rates generously (effective range ~85-97), so a mainstream bean we score ~6 will usually flag a divergence vs a 90+ critic. That flag is a feature: it surfaces the gap for your call.
_web_calibrate_help: Settings for the optional --web-calibrate lookup of external critic scores when a bean is not in the local CoffeeReview corpus. Best-effort, gated behind the flag, degrades to None on any error or when offline. Never blocks normal generation.
